title: AsBuiltJoints Object
description: The collection of as-built joints in this component. This provides access to all existing as-built joints and supports the ability to create new as-built joints.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates an AsBuiltJointInput object which is used to collect all of the information needed to create an as-built joint. This object is equivalent to the as-built joint dialog in the user-interface in that it doesn't represent an actual joint but just the information needed to create an as-built joint. Once this is fully defined the add method can be called, passing this object in to create the actual joint.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified as-built joint using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified as-built joint using a name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns number of joint origins in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: As-Built Joint Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new As-Built Joint.

add.title: AsBuiltJoints.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the new AsBuiltJoint object or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: An AsBuiltJointInput object that was created using the AsBuiltJoints.createInput method and then fully defined using the properties and methods on the AsBuiltJointInput object.

related_samples.name: As-Built Joint Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new As-Built Joint.

classType.title: AsBuiltJoints.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: AsBuiltJoints.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates an AsBuiltJointInput object which is used to collect all of the information needed to create an as-built joint. This object is equivalent to the as-built joint dialog in the user-interface in that it doesn't represent an actual joint but just the information needed to create an as-built joint. Once this is fully defined the add method can be called, passing this object in to create the actual joint.
createInput.return_description: Returns the new AsBuiltJointInput object or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: occurrenceOne
parameters.description: Specifies the first of two occurrences the joint is between.

parameters.name: occurrenceTwo
parameters.description: Specifies the second of two occurrences the joint is between.

parameters.name: geometry
parameters.description: Specifies the geometry of where the joint will be positioned. If the as-built joint is a rigid joint, this argument should be null because no geometry is needed.

related_samples.name: As-Built Joint Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new As-Built Joint.

item.title: AsBuiltJoints.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified as-built joint using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: AsBuiltJoints.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified as-built joint using a name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the item within the collection to return.

count.title: AsBuiltJoints.count Property
count.description: Returns number of joint origins in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = asBuiltJoints_var.count

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
